diagrams.title: Android Event Handling Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how user touch events flow from Activity through View hierarchy to event handlers
elements: User Touch → Activity.dispatchTouchEvent() → ViewGroup.onInterceptTouchEvent() → View.onTouch() → View.onTouchEvent() → Event Handlers (onClick, onLongClick)
diagrams.purpose: Understand event propagation and which method gets called at each stage

diagrams.title: Touch Event Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Timeline of a complete touch gesture showing all MotionEvent actions
elements: ACTION_DOWN (finger touches) → ACTION_MOVE (finger moves, can repeat) → ACTION_UP (finger lifts) or ACTION_CANCEL (interrupted)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the sequence of touch events for implementing drag/draw gestures

diagrams.title: Event Listener Registration Methods
diagrams.description: Three ways to register event listeners in Android
elements: 1. XML: android:onClick='method' → 2. Anonymous: view.setOnClickListener{...} → 3. Implements: Activity implements OnClickListener → Override onClick()
diagrams.purpose: Compare different approaches for handling user interactions
